diagnosed: No reproducible breakage in any path the mechanic can author. The CI-on-main failures that were red earlier today (775995c 08:50, 504d4af 07:37, c20ba0d 06:57, 6d343ae 04:05) were all the tests/profile-codec-golden.test.js byte-identity assertion, which was ALREADY FIXED by commit 38b7839f4 ('fix: codec-golden semantisk round-trip (ikke byte-identisk)') now in main — it swaps the impossible cross-impl DEFLATE byte-equality for a semantic round-trip; the last two main CI runs (197702266, 15aaacd4) are green. iOS Release's 2026-07-20 21:xx 'reached the maximum number of certificates' archive failures are resolved: WP-145's scripts/prune-signing-certs.js prune step is wired into ios-release.yml and the last four release runs succeeded. The one STILL-OPEN real issue is unchanged from 2026-07-20: bot-dispatched research escalations fail because anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 refuses a non-human-initiated run when allowed_bots is empty.
notes: HEALTH BASELINE this run: npm test = 820 passing (53 files); node scripts/validate-events.js = 59 events, 0 errors, 7 enriched; usage-state.json green (6% session, 14% week) so nothing is quota-attributable; sport fetcher files all non-empty/well-formed (cycling.json 99 bytes is the expected empty AI-research-filled stub, not a fetcher break). Recent runs on main all green: Static data pipeline (09:07, 08:53, 07:30), Research agent (06:41, 03:45), iOS Release (09:04, 08:50).

ESCALATION ISSUE — CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS: the 2026-07-20 abandoned log said the refused actor is github-actions[bot]. The failing run 29722743035 log shows otherwise: bot_name=claude[bot], bot_id=41898282 (the Claude GitHub App itself), ALLOWED_BOTS empty. So a fix scoped only to github-actions[bot] would NOT have unblocked it. escalate-research.js (WP-91) dispatches via `gh workflow run` with the job's real GITHUB_TOKEN, whose dispatched run can also surface as github-actions[bot], so BOTH actors must be allow-listed.

ATTEMPTED THE FIX, HOOK-BLOCKED (same wall as 2026-07-20): the only correct fix is in .github/workflows/research-agent.yml — a protected path. I branched (self-repair/20260721-0912) and tried to add `allowed_bots: "claude[bot],github-actions[bot]"` to all three anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 steps (Opus standard, Fable deep, Opus deep fallback), but the CI-only PreToolUse hook scripts/hooks/protect-automation.js blocked the Edit ('A human must author changes to these files'). Branch deleted, working tree clean, no PR opened. No non-protected-path alternative exists (allowed_bots is a claude-code-action input; the bot actor is inherent to a CI-issued dispatch).

FIX FOR A HUMAN (refined): in .github/workflows/research-agent.yml add `allowed_bots: "claude[bot],github-actions[bot]"` under `with:` on all three claude-code-action@v1 steps. Scoped (not "*") is safe and tight: this workflow's only bot-reachable trigger is workflow_dispatch, which already requires actions:write, and scheduled runs use a human actor so they were never affected. This closes the scout/coverage-critic -> research fast lane. Verify by dispatching one `-f tier=deep` escalation and confirming the run is no longer refused.
